cityDNA.coreFantasy: A North-African sun-cistern city where caravan routes, covered bazaars, command bastions, agricultural terraces, and sun-disc registry law make desert survival ceremonial and practical.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Qasr Siroth is not generic desert grandeur; the uploaded images show a bright, formal, road-and-cistern civilization of caravan reception, markets, command, and terrace food systems.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Qasr Siroth turns harsh light, cistern water, route hospitality, military order, market exchange, and terrace cultivation into a public order of Ma’at-like balance.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Polished white limestone, sandstone, stucco, brass lattice, linen shade, sun-disc reliefs, red awnings, caravan gates, blue civic lamps, command towers, covered bazaars, hanging gardens, terrace granaries, and bright desert plazas.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: North-African / Egyptian route-law culture centered on caravan hospitality, market order, military responsibility, terrace agriculture, and solar-registry authority.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Sun discs, cisterns, caravanserai gates, woven banners, covered bazaars, command standards, hanging gardens, seed halls, terrace granaries, and Aid Commons symbolize desert life held in balance.

canonLocks: Qasr Siroth is a mature North-African / Egyptian desert-cistern city, not a generic desert market.
The Wonder is North-African Sun-Cistern Citadel of Ma’at.
The Seat of Power is Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court.
The uploaded street sources cover four districts: Transit / Caravan / Rail, Grand Market, Military Command, and Agricultural Terraces.

Exact uploaded anchors include Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Waystation Hall Nook, Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook, Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Standard Tower Nook, Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Seed Hall Nook.

QSR-STREET-S5 image and sidecar are missing from this upload; hero sidecars show Aid Commons as a secondary major landmark, but exact S5 civic hall / monument / nook anchors are not locked from this batch.
provisionalCanon: Qasr Siroth's highest civic virtue is balanced provision.
Aid Commons likely belongs to the missing fifth district, but the missing S5 source remains a limitation rather than permission to invent details.
rejectedReads: pure palace city without water and food logistics
military command as decorative towers only
cautionFlags: Missing QSR S5 source: do not invent exact S5 minor anchors.
Keep cistern water, sun-disc law, caravan hospitality, command order, market trade, and terrace food production visible.
Preserve exact uploaded sidecar anchor names.

environment.environmentalLogic: Desert light, cistern water, shade, road access, and terrace food production operate as civic infrastructure rather than scenery.

forms: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
skyline: North-African Sun-Cistern Citadel of Ma’at as far wonder anchor
Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court as central authority
covered bazaar roofs, command towers, and terrace granaries stepping through the sunlit city

civilization.people: Citizens are route-literate, market-aware, law-conscious, and shaped by the practical discipline of water, shade, food, and safe passage.
civilization.governance: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court governs road access, market order, command obligations, water rights, food provision, and public petitions.
civilization.religion: Sun-disc reliefs, oath seals, cistern rites, caravan blessings, and Ma’at-like balance make practical desert order sacred-adjacent.

streets.appearance: Bright stone streets move between caravan gates, covered bazaars, command bastions, seed halls, terrace granaries, hanging gardens, and the sun-disc registry court.
streets.movement: Movement follows shaded bazaar lanes, caravan approaches, command courts, terrace stairs, cistern routes, and food-market paths.
streets.lighting: Hard sun is disciplined by linen shade, blue civic seals, brass reflection, and warm market lanterns.
streets.weatherEffects: Glare, dust wind, and dry heat make shade, cisterns, and terrace food systems central to city life.
streets.dayCycle: Caravan processing, bazaar trade, command duties, and terrace work dominate the day.
streets.nightCycle: Cistern checks, blue oath seals, guarded roads, and cooler market courts dominate the night.

housing.wealthy: Sunlit courtyard houses near the registry, command terraces, or caravan courts, with brass lattice and controlled shade.
housing.middle: Bazaar and terrace homes above shops or beside food routes, with shaded rooms and cistern access.
housing.poor: Road-edge housing and aid-adjacent quarters dependent on shared water, shade, and grain distribution.

districts.name: Transit / Caravan / Rail
districts.civicPurpose: Arrival, route law, guest rights, road security, and public signal response.
districts.visualIdentity: Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, Waystation Hall Nook.

districts.name: Grand Market
districts.civicPurpose: Public exchange, auction witnessing, shade-market order, and trade rumor.
districts.visualIdentity: Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, Auction Court Nook.

districts.name: Military Command
districts.civicPurpose: Road defense, command order, city protection, and visible authority.
districts.visualIdentity: Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, Standard Tower Nook.

districts.name: Agricultural Terraces
districts.civicPurpose: Food security, seed keeping, water use, and desert cultivation.
districts.visualIdentity: Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, Seed Hall Nook.

wonder.name: North-African Sun-Cistern Citadel of Ma’at
wonder.symbolicRole: Sun, cistern, law, balance, and desert survival made monumental.
wonder.visualRole: Far skyline citadel-wonder of white limestone, sunfire channels, and ceremonial water authority.
wonder.civicRole: Citywide rite, water memory, oath ceremony, and public balance symbol.
storyHooks: A sun-cistern rite reveals whether balance is real or just declared.

beacon.name: Caravanserai Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Public warning tied to arrival, road law, and hospitality.
beacon.visualRole: Beacon landmark rising from caravan infrastructure and road-facing courts.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates road closures, market shifts, command response, and food-route changes.
storyHooks: A Beacon change turns hospitality into a legal test.

seatOfPower.name: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Solar order, records, water law, and balanced judgment made government.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Central court with sun-disc reliefs, sealed archives, brass lattice, and public approach stairs.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Governs road rights, market disputes, military duties, water records, terrace food claims, and petitions.
storyHooks: A sun-disc record assigns water to the person the market forgot.

majorLandmarks.name: Covered Bazaar
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Commerce protected by shade and law.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Shaded market hall with awnings, auction court, and dense trade routes.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Trade, auction witnessing, and public exchange.
storyHooks: An auction court phrase reveals who controls the cistern routes.

majorLandmarks.name: Command Bastion
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Military order guarding route and water balance.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Command tower and court with standards, woven banners, and hard sunlit stone.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Road defense, command coordination, and crisis response.
storyHooks: A command order protects the road but threatens the granary.

majorLandmarks.name: Terrace Granary
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Food security made visible in the desert.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Stepped granary terraces with hanging gardens, seed hall, grain storage, and cistern-fed planting.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Food storage, seed keeping, and terrace agriculture.
storyHooks: A seed hall entry proves the city planned for a drought it publicly denied.

majorLandmarks.name: Aid Commons
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Public provision implied by hero-source coverage but missing district source detail.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Visible secondary landmark in hero views only; exact S5 street anchors are not available in this upload.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Probable public aid or provision function; must remain provisional until S5 source exists.
storyHooks: Aid Commons should not receive exact nook or monument treatment until the missing S5 source is supplied.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Transit / Caravan / Rail Civic Hall, Caravanserai Beacon, Woven Banners Monument, and Waystation Hall Nook.
S2 must show Grand Market Civic Hall, Covered Bazaar, Awning Monument, and Auction Court Nook.
S3 must show Military Command Hall, Command Bastion, Woven Banners Monument, and Standard Tower Nook.
S4 must show Agricultural Terraces Civic Hall, Terrace Granary, Hanging Gardens Monument, and Seed Hall Nook.
Do not author S5 district diorama specifics until QSR-STREET-S5 source material exists.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Keep North-African Sun-Cistern Citadel of Ma’at, Caravanserai Beacon, and Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court legible together.
Use sunlit white limestone, sandstone, stucco, brass lattice, linen shade, red awnings, cistern logic, caravan gates, command bastions, covered bazaars, and terrace granaries.
requiredVisualElements: Sun Disc Reliefs Sovereign Registry Court
Aid Commons as source-limited hero landmark
